name: Vaccinia_ITR_terminal_repeat
description: Vaccinia virus inverted terminal repeats (ITRs): ~10 kb identical sequences in head-to-head orientation at each end of the linear genome (range 6-12 kb depending on strain — WR has ~10 kb, Copenhagen ~12 kb, MVA shorter due to deletions). ITRs contain: (i) covalently-closed AT-rich incompletely-base-paired hairpin termini (~104 nt, exists in flip/flop A/B isomers), (ii) two sets of short tandem repeats (the ~70 bp 'TR70' and ~125 bp 'TR125' arrays, copy-number-variable across strains), (iii) the host-range gene K1L, ankyrin-repeat proteins, and the C/N gene families. ITR copy number variation drives genome-size differences between vaccinia strains; ITR sequence identity forces ambiguous mapping for short reads near either end.
expected_copy_count: 2 ITR copies per genome (left and right); internal tandem-repeat arrays variable across strains
